project.name: simtalk
project.description: Realtime speech-to-speech translation web app built around OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate, with LiveKit Cloud remote rooms.

nodes.name: Icons.tsx
nodes.summary: Defines an inline SVG icon library and a single STIcon component that renders branded glyphs (mic, swap, link, copy, chevrons, dots, etc.) by name with configurable size and color.

nodes.name: STIcon
nodes.summary: React component that looks up a named SVG path set and renders it inside a sized, color-customizable <svg> wrapper.

nodes.name: LanguagePicker.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal language picker UI: exposes a tappable LangCard trigger and a full-screen LanguagePickerSheet with search and filterable list backed by the LANGUAGES catalog.

nodes.name: LangCard
nodes.summary: Card-style button that displays a labelled language selection and opens the picker sheet when tapped.

nodes.name: LanguagePickerSheet
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet modal with debounced search, keyboard focus management, ESC-to-close, and filtered language rows that invoke onPick with the chosen Language.

nodes.name: ModeSegmented.tsx
nodes.summary: Segmented control for switching between the three SimTalk session modes (listener, practice, turnabout), rendered as icon+label buttons that emit onChange.

nodes.name: ModeSegmented
nodes.summary: Renders a horizontal row of mode toggles, highlighting the active value and invoking onChange when a different mode is selected.

nodes.name: VideoTile.tsx
nodes.summary: Remote-room video tile that attaches a LiveKit video track to a <video> element, falls back to an initial avatar, and overlays language, mic, and speaking-state pills.

nodes.name: Pill
nodes.summary: Internal pill-shaped overlay used by VideoTile to show language, mic state, and speaker hints in a consistent style.

nodes.name: VideoTile
nodes.summary: Main video tile component: binds a LiveKit VideoTrack via attach/detach lifecycle, renders avatar fallback, mic-toggle button, and language/speaking overlays for a participant.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useEffect with attach/detach to bind imperative LiveKit MediaStreamTrack to a declarative React <video> element.

nodes.name: languages.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical BCP-47 language catalog used across SimTalk: exports AUTO_LANGUAGE sentinel, the LANGUAGES list, and findLanguage/isAutoLanguage lookup helpers.

nodes.name: findLanguage
nodes.summary: Returns the Language entry whose code matches the given BCP-47 string, or undefined when not found.

nodes.name: isAutoLanguage
nodes.summary: Predicate that reports whether a Language entry is the AUTO_LANGUAGE sentinel used to trigger source-language auto-detection.

nodes.name: primitives.tsx
nodes.summary: Core SimTalk brand primitives: design tokens (ST palette, fonts, NEBULA gradient) and reusable presentational components (STButton, STCard, STTitle, STChip, STPill, LiveDot).
nodes.languageNotes: Inline style objects rather than a CSS-in-JS runtime; relies on global CSS variables for theming.

nodes.name: STButton
nodes.summary: Branded button primitive supporting primary/secondary variants, sizes, optional leading icon, and a press-down animation that respects mouse lifecycle handlers.

nodes.name: STCard
nodes.summary: Card surface primitive with configurable tone (white/navy/etc), padding, and optional click handler used for grouped content blocks.

nodes.name: STTitle
nodes.summary: Stroked/shadowed display heading primitive that renders as a configurable tag and applies the SimTalk display font with adjustable size and color.

nodes.name: STChip
nodes.summary: Inline chip primitive with optional icon and stroke used for metadata labels (language, status hints) on a colored background.

nodes.name: STPill
nodes.summary: Solid pill badge primitive with an optional leading icon used for prominent labels such as LIVE indicators and counts.

nodes.name: LiveDot
nodes.summary: Animated pulsing dot used to signal a live/streaming state, with color configurable via the SimTalk token palette.

nodes.name: ListenerSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: Listener-mode screen that displays incoming translated speech as live captions plus a rolling sentence history, with controls to pause streaming and open the full transcript sheet.

nodes.name: ListenerSurface
nodes.summary: Renders the listener UI: derives the latest two sentences from streaming text, shows recent history entries, and exposes pause and transcript-open actions.

nodes.name: Lobby.tsx
nodes.summary: Pre-session lobby screen for choosing mode, source/target languages, swapping them, and launching either a local OpenAI translation session or a LiveKit remote room.

nodes.name: Lobby
nodes.summary: Lobby root component: manages picker visibility, validates mode-vs-auto-language combinations, and wires language pickers, mode segmented control, and the launch/create-room CTAs.

nodes.name: PracticeSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: Practice-mode screen that captures the learner's spoken attempt, compares it against the model translation using token-level diff, and offers reveal/try-again/next controls.

nodes.name: diff
nodes.summary: Tokenizes the learner's attempt and the reference translation, then returns a per-token comparison marking each word as matched or missing.

nodes.name: PracticeSurface
nodes.summary: Coordinates the practice flow stages (recording, comparing, reviewing): renders heard text, attempt input, diff feedback, and orchestrates recording start/stop and submission callbacks.

nodes.name: RemoteRoomSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: LiveKit remote-room screen: renders local and remote video tiles, translation captions, join/leave controls, mic/camera toggles, language pickers, and copy-link sharing.

nodes.name: PageShell
nodes.summary: Internal layout wrapper that provides the nebula background and constrained main column used by the remote-room screen.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Static header bar for the remote-room screen showing SimTalk branding and room context.

nodes.name: RemoteRoomSurface
nodes.summary: Top-level remote-room component: orchestrates participant tiles, translation captions, original-audio mute, mic/camera toggles, language pickers, and copy-link sharing for a LiveKit session.

nodes.name: Summary.tsx
nodes.summary: Post-session summary screen that reviews mode, languages, transcripts, and per-turn history, with copy, download, and new-session actions.

nodes.name: Summary
nodes.summary: Renders the session recap: input/output transcripts, per-turn list, optional audio download link, and CTAs for copying, downloading transcript, or starting a new session.

nodes.name: TranscriptSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet overlay that lists transcript entries with copy-all support and ESC-to-close keyboard handling.

nodes.name: TranscriptSheet
nodes.summary: Renders a scrollable list of transcript entries inside a modal sheet, wiring ESC-key dismissal, click-outside close, and a copy-all action.

nodes.name: TurnaboutSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: Turnabout-mode screen for in-person bilingual conversations: shows alternating speaker bubbles, live source/target captions, and a push-to-talk mic with side flip control.

nodes.name: SpeakerBadge
nodes.summary: Renders a speaker badge that identifies which language side is currently active and indicates speaking state with a live dot.

nodes.name: Bubble
nodes.summary: Conversation bubble that pairs a source utterance with its translated counterpart for one turn in the turnabout transcript.

nodes.name: TurnaboutSurface
nodes.summary: Coordinates the turnabout UI: renders the turn list with auto-scroll, live source/destination captions, the language flip toggle, and the press-and-hold mic with pointer capture.

nodes.name: DevDrawer.tsx
nodes.summary: Developer debug drawer that surfaces session token expiry, connection status, recording status, and recent transcript deltas; opens/closes via ESC and click-outside.

nodes.name: DevDrawer
nodes.summary: Renders the dev drawer overlay with token/session expiry timestamps, status rows, and a scrollable list of recent transcript deltas for debugging.

nodes.name: RemoteRoomSurface.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest + Testing Library component test for RemoteRoomSurface, asserting it renders the expected room UI and reacts to language changes.

nodes.name: STButton.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest + Testing Library smoke test verifying that the STButton primitive renders its children correctly.

nodes.name: CODEMAP.json
nodes.summary: Generated structural codemap of the repository produced by the Agentic OS tooling; serves as the fallback graph when the Understand-Anything provider is unavailable. Auto-generated and not edited by hand.
nodes.languageNotes: Large machine-generated JSON keyed by schema_version, generated_at, root, and entries; treated as opaque by routing tooling.

nodes.name: GRAPH_INDEX.md
nodes.summary: Records Agentic OS graph status: which provider is active (understand-anything), the graph path, freshness timestamps, parseability, coverage, and human routing notes about provider quirks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses managed/human comment fences (`<!-- agentic:managed:start -->`, `<!-- human:notes:start -->`) to separate auto-maintained sections from human-edited notes.

nodes.name: MEMORY_INDEX.md
nodes.summary: Top-level Agentic OS memory index: lists subsystem memory files, high-risk security/secrets areas, source-of-truth files, lessons/decisions pointers, and human-curated routing hints used to map task intent to relevant code paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines machine-managed sections (subsystems, freshness, graph status) with a human notes block of routing hints and priority warnings.

nodes.name: PROJECT_BRIEF.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project brief for SimTalk: purpose, stack, Phase 1/2 deployment plan, major subsystems, source-of-truth files, conflicts, unknowns, plus human-authored product intent, architectural philosophy, and business constraints.
nodes.languageNotes: Anchors agent context — references PRD.md, System_Architecture.md, README.md, CLAUDE.md, and shared Zod contracts as the authoritative inputs.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index for the Agentic OS subsystems folder, explaining its role as agent-first reference notes per subsystem and listing the five SimTalk subsystems (api, web, shared, tests, infra).

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem reference for the Hono backend token-minting service, documenting owned paths under backend/, the /realtime/token contract, dependencies on OpenAI and shared Zod schemas, and Phase 1 security invariants.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the standard subsystem template: Purpose, Owned paths, Public contracts, Source-of-truth files, Dependencies, Invariants, Failure modes, Do-not-do rules, Lessons, Unknowns.

nodes.name: infra.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem reference for repository infrastructure and tooling, covering pnpm workspace layout, root scripts, TypeScript configs, Vitest/Playwright setup, env templates, and CI/deployment surface.

nodes.name: shared.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem reference for the @simtalk/shared-types package, defining Zod schemas as the cross-boundary source of truth for request/response shapes, modes, languages, and API errors.

nodes.name: tests.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem reference for the centralised tests/ directory, documenting the unit/integration/component/e2e layout, Vitest and Playwright tooling, and the test-structure skill governing organisation.

nodes.name: web.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem reference for the React 19 + Vite 7 frontend, documenting the realtime token client, WebRTC translation session lifecycle, UI surfaces, design tokens, and accessibility invariants.

nodes.name: agentic.json
nodes.summary: Agentic OS configuration declaring graph paths, freshness rules, ignore globs, test discovery patterns, high-risk hotspots, and subsystem keyword maps used by the in-repo task router.

nodes.name: decisions.md
nodes.summary: Architectural decision log capturing Phase 1 choices: Hono backend, Vite/React frontend, OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate over WebRTC, no database, browser-local recording, Vercel deployment, and the backend access gate.

nodes.name: incidents.md
nodes.summary: Incident log file containing only the template entry; no production incidents have been recorded yet.

nodes.name: settings.local.json
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code permission overrides that whitelist specific shell commands and tool invocations for the assistant in this repo.

nodes.name: phase_one_implementation_efb87251.plan.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 implementation plan covering grounding context, invariants, open unknowns, implementation slices, and the recommended first coding pass for the realtime translation app.

nodes.name: simtalk_deployment_rooms_b4ffca08.plan.md
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment and Phase 1.5 remote-rooms plan covering security hardening, LiveKit integration, CI/CD workflow, required env vars, and a browser test checklist.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Cursor skill describing the canonical /tests/ structure for the repo and the discovery, classification, migration, and verification steps to keep tests centralized.

nodes.name: .prettierignore
nodes.summary: Prettier ignore patterns excluding build output, dependency caches, generated artifacts, and lockfiles from formatting passes.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything ignore file scoping the knowledge graph to relevant source paths and excluding build output, caches, and generated artifacts.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Vercel serverless entry that mounts the Hono backend app under /api and exports a Node request listener via @hono/node-server.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the api/ Vercel function directory, extending the backend tsconfig and scoping compilation to api sources.

nodes.name: app.ts
nodes.summary: Composes the Hono backend application by wiring CORS, security headers, the access gate, and mounting the health, realtime, and rooms routes alongside shared error handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: Factory-style createApp accepts an injected AppConfig and dependency bag, which keeps the app testable without monkey-patching.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Parses process environment variables into a typed AppConfig (port, allowed origins, OpenAI and LiveKit credentials, rate limits, TTLs) with bounded defaults and an environment guard for production access passwords.

nodes.name: accessGate.ts
nodes.summary: Hono middleware enforcing an X-Access-Password header against configured passwords using SHA-256 hashing and timing-safe comparison, returning 401 on failure.

nodes.name: cors.ts
nodes.summary: Hono CORS middleware that reflects an allowed origin from the configured whitelist, sets standard CORS headers, and short-circuits OPTIONS preflight with 204.

nodes.name: rateLimit.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory fixed-window rate limiter middleware keyed by forwarded client IP, with pruning of expired entries and standard 429 responses including Retry-After.

nodes.name: securityHeaders.ts
nodes.summary: Adds the standard hardening response headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options) to every response after downstream handlers run.

nodes.name: health.ts
nodes.summary: Read-only GET / health route returning a Zod-validated health payload with service name and timestamp for liveness probes.

nodes.name: realtime.ts
nodes.summary: POST /realtime/token Hono route that validates the client request, mints an OpenAI realtime translation client secret via the service layer, and maps upstream errors to consistent API error responses.

nodes.name: rooms.ts
nodes.summary: Hono routes that create LiveKit rooms and mint short-lived participant tokens, validating room ids and request bodies and translating LiveKit errors to typed API error responses.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Node entry point that boots the Hono application on the configured port via @hono/node-server.

nodes.name: liveKitRooms.ts
nodes.summary: LiveKit Cloud service that lazily constructs a RoomServiceClient, ensures the requested room exists with the configured timeouts, and signs short-lived participant JWTs via AccessToken, with injectable seams for tests.
nodes.languageNotes: All side effects sit behind injected dependencies (now, roomService, tokenSigner, randomId) so unit tests can run without the real LiveKit SDK.

nodes.name: openAiRealtime.ts
nodes.summary: Calls OpenAI's realtime translation client-secret endpoint with an AbortController-based timeout, normalizes the response into the shared realtime token schema, and throws a typed OpenAiRealtimeError on missing config or upstream failures.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses AbortController + setTimeout to bound the upstream call and a discriminated error 'kind' field so the route layer can map failures to specific HTTP responses.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the backend package that aliases @simtalk/shared-types to the in-repo source and discovers tests from the central /tests/backend directory.

nodes.name: createApp
nodes.summary: Builds and returns a Hono app instance: applies CORS and security middleware, mounts the health route, gates /realtime and /rooms behind the access middleware, mounts the realtime and rooms routes, and installs notFound and onError handlers that emit validated apiError payloads.

nodes.name: createAppConfig
nodes.summary: Reads process.env (or an injected env) and returns a frozen AppConfig with parsed ports, allowed origins, OpenAI and LiveKit credentials, rate limits, and TTLs, throwing if the access password is missing outside development.

nodes.name: createAccessGateMiddleware
nodes.summary: Returns a Hono middleware that requires the X-Access-Password header to match one of the configured password hashes using SHA-256 and timing-safe comparison, no-opping when no passwords are configured.

nodes.name: createCorsMiddleware
nodes.summary: Returns a Hono CORS middleware that echoes the request Origin only when present in the allowed list and responds 204 to OPTIONS preflight requests.

nodes.name: createRateLimitMiddleware
nodes.summary: Returns a Hono middleware that tracks request counts per client key in an in-memory map, prunes expired entries, and returns a 429 with Retry-After when the per-window quota is exceeded.

nodes.name: clientKeyFromHeaders
nodes.summary: Derives a stable rate-limit key from request headers, preferring the last x-forwarded-for hop, then x-real-ip, and finally a 'local' fallback.

nodes.name: pruneEntries
nodes.summary: Mutates the rate-limit map to evict entries past their reset time and trims the map down to maxEntries by oldest insertion order.

nodes.name: securityHeaders
nodes.summary: Hono middleware that appends the standard hardening response headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options) after downstream handlers complete.

nodes.name: createRealtimeRoute
nodes.summary: Returns a Hono sub-app exposing POST /token that rate-limits requests, validates the body against the realtime token schema, calls the OpenAI realtime service, and maps OpenAiRealtimeError kinds to 503 or 502 API error responses.

nodes.name: createRoomsRoute
nodes.summary: Returns a Hono sub-app exposing POST / to create a LiveKit room and POST /:roomId/token to mint a participant token, validating request data with shared Zod schemas and translating LiveKitRoomError kinds into typed API error responses.

nodes.name: LiveKitRoomError
nodes.summary: Typed Error subclass carrying a discriminated 'kind' (missing_config, upstream_unavailable, invalid_request) so the rooms route can branch its HTTP response on the LiveKit failure category.

nodes.name: createLiveKitRoomService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a LiveKit room service with createRoom and createParticipantToken methods that lazily instantiate a RoomServiceClient, ensure the room exists with configured timeouts, and sign short-lived AccessToken JWTs.

nodes.name: assertLiveKitConfig
nodes.summary: Validates that the LiveKit URL, API key, and API secret are all present in config and returns them as a strictly typed object, throwing a missing_config LiveKitRoomError otherwise.

nodes.name: defaultTokenSigner
nodes.summary: Default token signer that builds a LiveKit AccessToken with the requested identity, display name, and TTL, grants room join with publish/subscribe permissions, and returns a signed JWT.

nodes.name: OpenAiRealtimeError
nodes.summary: Typed Error subclass carrying a discriminated 'kind' (missing_config, upstream_unavailable, invalid_upstream_response) so the realtime route can branch its HTTP response on the upstream failure category.

nodes.name: createOpenAiRealtimeService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service whose createTranslationClientSecret method posts a translation session request to OpenAI with an AbortController timeout, surfaces missing config and upstream failures as OpenAiRealtimeError, and parses successful responses into the shared token schema.

nodes.name: parseOpenAiClientSecretResponse
nodes.summary: Validates and reshapes the OpenAI client-secret response into a RealtimeTokenResponse, throwing OpenAiRealtimeError on missing secret, session id, or invalid expiry timestamps.

nodes.name: unixSecondsToIso
nodes.summary: Converts a Unix-seconds timestamp into an ISO-8601 string, throwing an invalid_upstream_response OpenAiRealtimeError when the value is missing or not a finite number.

nodes.name: 2026-05-24-access-gate.md
nodes.summary: Detailed Superpowers implementation plan for the password-protected access gate covering backend middleware, frontend modal flow, tests, and deployment guardrails.

nodes.name: 2026-05-26-they-speak-auto-default.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for making the 'they speak' mode the default selection in remote rooms with auto-language detection.

nodes.name: 2026-05-24-access-gate-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the access-gate feature describing the shared-password flow, cookie semantics, and frontend gate modal contract.

nodes.name: 2026-05-26-they-speak-auto-default-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for defaulting the remote-room mode to 'they speak' with automatic source-language detection.

nodes.name: ux-redesign-plan.md
nodes.summary: UX redesign plan describing the SimTalk brand system, screen layouts, and progressive disclosure approach across modes.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: Flat ESLint configuration combining @eslint/js recommended, typescript-eslint, and react-hooks rules with monorepo-wide ignore patterns.

nodes.name: site.webmanifest
nodes.summary: PWA web app manifest declaring icons, theme color, and display mode for the SimTalk frontend.

nodes.name: AccessGateModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dialog that prompts the user for the shared access-gate password before the rest of the app becomes usable, with focus management and Escape-to-close handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses inline style objects keyed off SimTalk brand primitives instead of a CSS framework.

nodes.name: AccessGateModal
nodes.summary: React component that renders the access-gate password form, wires up keyboard handling, and forwards submit/close events to its parent.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet defining SimTalk brand CSS custom properties (colors, spacing, typography) and base resets imported once from the app entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: CSS custom properties drive theme switching and accessibility-aware reduced-motion handling.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite + Vitest configuration for the frontend package: React plugin, monorepo path aliases, dev/preview ports, and jsdom test setup pointing at the shared tests/frontend tree.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright configuration for repo-wide end-to-end tests targeting the frontend dev server on 127.0.0.1:5173 with Chromium and CI-aware retries.

nodes.name: build_codemap.py
nodes.summary: Generates the legacy .agentic/CODEMAP.json by walking the repository, classifying files into subsystems, tagging risk, and emitting deterministic entries.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard-library only; deterministic output to keep diffs minimal across runs.

nodes.name: _walk
nodes.summary: Walks the project tree honouring ignore patterns and yields candidate file paths for codemap entry building.

nodes.name: _build_entries
nodes.summary: Builds the per-file codemap entry list, including language detection, subsystem tagging, risk tagging, and related-test discovery.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that loads config, builds entries, and writes the JSON codemap to disk with an entry count summary.

nodes.name: graph_sync.py
nodes.summary: Refreshes the .agentic/GRAPH_INDEX.md managed region by sampling graph paths, counting nodes, and recording staleness signals for Agentic OS routing.

nodes.name: _sample_paths_resolve
nodes.summary: Resolves the configured graph sample paths to absolute files, returning existence and freshness data per candidate.

nodes.name: _build_managed_block
nodes.summary: Builds the managed markdown block describing graph provider, node count, and last-sync timestamp for GRAPH_INDEX.md.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that loads config, resolves graph paths, rebuilds the managed block, and splices it into GRAPH_INDEX.md in place.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Quick-reference for the Agentic OS Python tooling describing each script's role and the canonical invocation order.

nodes.name: route_task.py
nodes.summary: Core Agentic OS routing engine that turns a free-form task description into a graph-aware context bundle of files, tests, subsystems, and risks.
nodes.languageNotes: Graph-first routing with codemap and filesystem fallbacks; user-named anchors always override heuristics.

nodes.name: _index_graph
nodes.summary: Indexes graph nodes by id, type, and path so subsequent scoring and anchor lookups can resolve them in constant time.

nodes.name: _node_score
nodes.summary: Computes a relevance score for a graph node against expanded task tokens, weighting summary, tags, and path matches.

nodes.name: _explicit_anchors_from_graph
nodes.summary: Promotes user-named paths from the task text into explicit graph anchors that bypass heuristic routing.

nodes.name: _expand_dependencies
nodes.summary: Expands the seed node set along graph edges to include direct dependencies and dependents up to a bounded depth.

nodes.name: _related_tests_for
nodes.summary: Finds tests related to the selected source files using configured test-discovery globs with a tests/ directory fallback.

nodes.name: _codemap_routing
nodes.summary: Legacy codemap-based routing path used when the graph provider is unavailable, scoring CODEMAP.json entries against the task.

nodes.name: _compute_confidence
nodes.summary: Computes a confidence score for the emitted bundle based on graph availability, anchor presence, and selection breadth.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that orchestrates graph load, anchor resolution, scoring, expansion, related-test discovery, and bundle emission.

nodes.name: test_smoke.py
nodes.summary: Unittest smoke suite that loads the Agentic OS scripts via importlib and exercises their CLI contracts against the live repository.

nodes.name: _CwdRepoRoot
nodes.summary: Context manager that temporarily changes the working directory to the repo root for the duration of a test.

nodes.name: ValidateMemorySmoke
nodes.summary: Smoke tests for validate_memory.py covering clean execution and the no-context-cache-dir invariant.

nodes.name: RouteTaskSmoke
nodes.summary: Smoke tests for route_task.py covering v2 bundle contract, user-named anchors, and graph-aware routing behaviour.

nodes.name: RelatedTestsUnit
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the related-tests discovery logic in route_task.py, exercising configured globs and the tests/ fallback.

nodes.name: GlobMatchUnit
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the glob-to-regex matcher in route_task.py, including double-star and segment-crossing behaviour.

nodes.name: update_memory.py
nodes.summary: Refreshes Agentic OS operational memory by running graph_sync, inspecting git changes, and updating the freshness/marker blocks.

nodes.name: _run_graph_sync
nodes.summary: Loads graph_sync.py via importlib and invokes its main() in-process so the working directory and stdout are shared.

nodes.name: _git_changed_files
nodes.summary: Returns the list of project-relative files changed since the last update_memory marker, using the git CLI when available.

nodes.name: _refresh_freshness_block
nodes.summary: Rebuilds the managed freshness block in MEMORY_INDEX.md based on subsystem activity and risk tags from recent changes.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that orchestrates graph sync, git change inspection, freshness refresh, and marker persistence.

nodes.name: validate_memory.py
nodes.summary: Validates Agentic OS memory and graph artifacts, reporting errors and warnings for missing files, stale data, broken markers, and orphan context caches.

nodes.name: Report
nodes.summary: Simple aggregator class collecting validation errors and warnings during a single validate_memory run.

nodes.name: _check_graph
nodes.summary: Validates the configured graph provider/path, presence of GRAPH_INDEX.md, and freshness of the underlying graph artifact.

nodes.name: _check_region_markers
nodes.summary: Checks that managed-region markers in memory files are paired correctly and not crossing file boundaries.

nodes.name: _check_context_cache
nodes.summary: Detects and reports the disallowed .agentic/CONTEXT cache directory, which should never be created by Agentic OS tooling.

nodes.name: _check_sample_paths
nodes.summary: Checks that configured sample paths in agentic.json resolve to real files, surfacing stale config entries.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs every validation check and reports aggregated errors and warnings with a non-zero exit on failure.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the @simtalk/shared-types package wiring up Node-environment unit tests for the Zod schemas.

nodes.name: accessGate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the access-gate middleware in the assembled backend app, covering cookie issuance, password failure, and protected-route behaviour.

nodes.name: app.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the composed Hono app verifying middleware wiring, route registration, and global response headers.

nodes.name: realtime.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for POST /realtime/token covering request validation, OpenAI client-secret minting, error sanitization, and no-store caching.

nodes.name: rooms.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the LiveKit rooms routes covering room creation, token issuance, and error mapping from the LiveKit service.

nodes.name: vercelAdapter.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test for the Vercel entry adapter ensuring api/index.ts exports the composed app handler.

nodes.name: config.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for createAppConfig covering required env vars, default ports, CORS parsing, and access-gate password validation.

nodes.name: AccessGateModal.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Component tests for AccessGateModal covering rendering, error display, submission of trimmed codes, and close handling.

nodes.name: accessGate.ts
nodes.summary: Browser sessionStorage helpers for the access-gate password plus an AccessDeniedError used to signal 401 responses from token endpoints.

nodes.name: getStoredPassword
nodes.summary: Returns the access-gate password cached in sessionStorage, or null when none is stored.

nodes.name: setStoredPassword
nodes.summary: Persists the access-gate password to sessionStorage for the current tab lifetime.

nodes.name: clearStoredPassword
nodes.summary: Removes the cached access-gate password from sessionStorage after a 401 or sign-out.

nodes.name: AccessDeniedError
nodes.summary: Custom Error subclass thrown by token clients when the backend rejects a request with HTTP 401, so UI can prompt for the gate password.

nodes.name: liveKitRemoteRoomSession.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a LiveKit remote room session: joins via a minted room token, manages local mic/camera tracks, subscribes to the remote participant's audio/video, and hands the original audio stream to a realtime translation session.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large factory function exposes a controller object with start/stop/setMicrophoneEnabled/setCameraEnabled/setLocalYouHear hooks; relies heavily on LiveKit RoomEvent listeners.

nodes.name: createLiveKitRemoteRoomSession
nodes.summary: Factory that wires a LiveKit Room with token minting, local mic/camera control, remote participant tracking, and realtime translation handoff, returning a controller for UI use.

nodes.name: realtimeTokenClient.ts
nodes.summary: Browser client that POSTs to the backend /realtime/token endpoint, attaching the access-gate password and validating the response with shared Zod schemas before returning a short-lived OpenAI realtime client secret.

nodes.name: RealtimeTokenClientError
nodes.summary: Typed error thrown when the realtime token request fails validation, network, or backend-level checks, carrying an error code and optional status.

nodes.name: requestRealtimeToken
nodes.summary: Validates the request, calls the backend /realtime/token endpoint with the access-gate password, handles 401/timeout/error cases, and returns a Zod-validated realtime token response.

nodes.name: realtimeTranslationSession.ts
nodes.summary: Owns the browser-side WebRTC realtime translation session: captures or accepts a mic stream, exchanges SDP directly with the OpenAI realtime endpoint using a minted client secret, surfaces transcript deltas, and plays back translated audio.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses AbortSignal-aware `abortable` helper to make every async step cancellable without leaking peer connections or tracks.

nodes.name: RealtimeTranslationSessionError
nodes.summary: Typed error raised by the realtime translation session for SDP, network, or media failures so callers can react appropriately.

nodes.name: parseTranscriptDelta
nodes.summary: Best-effort parser for realtime data-channel messages that extracts the transcript text and isFinal flag from OpenAI realtime delta events.

nodes.name: abortable
nodes.summary: Wraps a promise so it rejects with the AbortSignal's reason if aborted mid-flight, used to make SDP/media setup steps cancellable.

nodes.name: createRealtimeTranslationSession
nodes.summary: Factory that builds an RTCPeerConnection, attaches the local/forwarded mic stream, exchanges an SDP offer with the OpenAI realtime endpoint, and returns a controller that exposes stop and audio enable/disable.

nodes.name: roomTokenClient.ts
nodes.summary: Browser client for the backend room API: creates new LiveKit rooms and mints scoped room tokens, attaching the access-gate password and validating responses with shared Zod schemas.

nodes.name: RoomTokenClientError
nodes.summary: Typed error thrown by room create/token requests carrying an error code and optional HTTP status for UI handling.

nodes.name: parseApiError
nodes.summary: Reads a non-2xx response body, validates it against the shared apiErrorSchema, and returns a RoomTokenClientError preserving the backend's error code or a fallback.

nodes.name: requestRoomCreate
nodes.summary: Calls POST /rooms with the access-gate password, handles 401 by clearing the cached password, and returns a Zod-validated roomCreateResponse.

nodes.name: requestRoomToken
nodes.summary: Validates roomId and request, calls POST /rooms/:id/token, handles 401, and returns a Zod-validated room access token for joining the LiveKit room.

nodes.name: accessGate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for sessionStorage password helpers and the AccessDeniedError constructor in accessGate.

nodes.name: liveKitRemoteRoomSession.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests that drive createLiveKitRemoteRoomSession against a fake LiveKit Room, verifying token minting, mic/camera control, remote participant handling, and translation handoff.

nodes.name: createFakeRoom
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a minimal fake LiveKit Room with vi.fn mocks for connect/disconnect/setMicrophoneEnabled/setCameraEnabled and a handler map for RoomEvent subscriptions.

nodes.name: realtimeTokenClient.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering validation, gate-password headers, 401 clearing, timeout handling, and schema validation in requestRealtimeToken.

nodes.name: realtimeTranslationSession.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for createRealtimeTranslationSession using fake MediaStream, RTCPeerConnection and DataChannel doubles to verify SDP exchange, transcript delta parsing, abort handling, and teardown.

nodes.name: roomTokenClient.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for room create/token requests covering header injection, 401 password clearing, error parsing, and schema validation.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level React component that orchestrates the entire SimTalk experience: lobby selection, access gate, practice/turnabout/listener/remote-room session lifecycles, transcript buffers, MediaRecorder capture, and routing between screens.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large function component with extensive useState/useRef/useCallback hooks; manages multiple async session subsystems (OpenAI Realtime, LiveKit, recorder) from one place.

nodes.name: SessionHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Sticky session header showing mode, language pair, active speaker, live elapsed timer, status indicator, and exit/dev-drawer controls during an active translation session.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Vite entry point that mounts the React App component into #root inside StrictMode and loads the global design tokens stylesheet.

nodes.name: roomIdFromPathname
nodes.summary: Parses the current window pathname for a `/rooms/<id>` segment and returns the decoded room id, or null when the route is not a remote-room URL.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component that wires lobby/practice/turnabout/listener/remote-room views together, owns transcript state, manages access-gate flow, and drives realtime translation and LiveKit session lifecycles.
nodes.languageNotes: Encapsulates ~40 useState/useRef/useCallback hooks and several useEffect cleanup handlers in a single function — refactor candidate.

nodes.name: SessionHeader
nodes.summary: Sticky in-session header that displays the mode and language pair, ticks a 1s elapsed timer, surfaces the current status, and exposes exit and dev-drawer toggle buttons.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for cross-boundary contracts: Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types for realtime token, LiveKit room create/token, API errors, health, and translation modes shared by the backend and frontend.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod for runtime validation and exports inferred TypeScript types via z.infer so request/response shapes stay synchronized.

nodes.name: App.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest component test suite for the frontend App, mocking fetch, MediaRecorder, and RTCPeerConnection to exercise the access gate flow, token minting, LiveKit room provisioning, and translation session lifecycle.
nodes.languageNotes: Stubs WebRTC and MediaRecorder browser APIs via vi.stubGlobal to drive realistic UI flows without a real browser audio pipeline.

nodes.name: index.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering shared Zod schemas in shared/types — validates conversation modes, language codes, token request/response shapes, room creation, and API error envelopes.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on every push and PR, executing format, lint, typecheck, unit tests, build, and Playwright end-to-end tests on Node 22 with pnpm 10.16.1.
nodes.languageNotes: Pins third-party actions by full commit SHA and disables persist-credentials on checkout for supply-chain hardening.

nodes.name: vercel-deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions deployment workflow gated on CI success that publishes Vercel preview builds for non-main pushes and production builds for main, using protected environments with Vercel CLI pull/build/deploy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses workflow_run trigger with branch-based conditional jobs and environment-scoped secrets to separate preview from production deploys.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Repo-orientation guide for AI coding assistants, summarising the pnpm workspace layout, commands, three-boundary realtime translation architecture, hard Phase 1 invariants, and frontend conventions.

nodes.name: PRD.md
nodes.summary: Product Requirements Document defining SimTalk's vision, target users, MVP/Phase 2 scope, success metrics, non-goals, key risks, and product principles for the realtime translation app.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project README covering status, tech stack, architecture, security/privacy, project structure, local development, environment variables, API usage, testing, Vercel deployment, standards, roadmap, and decision log.

nodes.name: System_Architecture.md
nodes.summary: System architecture specification describing the Phase 1 token-minting backend and direct browser-to-OpenAI Realtime flow, plus Phase 1.5 private rooms and Phase 2 multi-user LiveKit architecture.

nodes.name: agentic_os_plan_v2.md
nodes.summary: In-depth manifesto and reference implementation plan for the Agentic OS — the in-repo memory/routing system under .agentic/ and scripts/agentic/ — covering principles, skills, tooling, repo structure, phased rollout, and design guardrails.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui generator configuration (style, Tailwind, RSC, TSX, path aliases, icon library); per CLAUDE.md this is a stale scaffold remnant since Tailwind and shadcn are not actually installed in the active frontend.
nodes.languageNotes: Tracked but unused — flagged stale by CLAUDE.md alongside frontend/src/components/ui/ and frontend/src/lib/utils.ts.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root workspace manifest pinning pnpm 10 and Node 22 engines, defining repo-level dev/build/test/typecheck/e2e scripts that fan out across the shared-types, backend, and frontend packages.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace declaration enumerating the three packages (backend, frontend, shared/types) that participate in the monorepo.

nodes.name: tsconfig.base.json
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript base config establishing strict mode and common compiler options inherited by the backend, frontend, and shared-types package tsconfigs.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration setting the build/install commands, Vite framework, frontend output directory, SPA rewrites, and required security response headers (CSP, HSTS, frame/MIME/referrer policies).

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template documenting all backend runtime environment variables: OpenAI realtime credentials, LiveKit room credentials, allowed origins, rate-limit windows, and the optional app access password.
nodes.languageNotes: Eighteen KEY=VALUE entries spanning OpenAI realtime, LiveKit, CORS, rate-limit, and access-gate settings — safe template counterpart to backend/.env.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Manifest for the @simtalk/backend workspace package; declares Hono, @hono/node-server, livekit-server-sdk, and the shared-types workspace dep, plus dev/build/test scripts (tsx watch, tsc, vitest).

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Production TypeScript compiler config for the backend; extends the repo base, targets ES2022 NodeNext modules, emits declarations and source maps from src/ to dist/, and excludes test files.

nodes.name: tsconfig.test.json
nodes.summary: Test-only TypeScript config that extends backend/tsconfig.json, adds DOM lib and Vitest path aliases, includes the repo-level tests/backend tree, and runs in noEmit type-check mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses paths aliases to resolve @simtalk/shared-types directly to the workspace source so type-checking does not require a prior shared-types build.

nodes.name: 00_repo_context.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated repository context snapshot covering git state, project state, source files reviewed, and configuration/environment for the SimTalk codebase.

nodes.name: 01_architecture_map.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated architecture and tech-stack map describing runtime flows, entrypoints, storage, tests/CI, and remaining uncertainties for the realtime translation system.

nodes.name: 02_folder_structure.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated folder-structure map of the monorepo and notable drift between expected and actual layout.

nodes.name: 03_file_index.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated index categorising every tracked source/config/test/script file across root docs, the Vercel adapter, backend, shared types, frontend, tests, and Agentic OS tooling.

nodes.name: 04_readme_gap_analysis.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated README gap analysis classifying the project, auditing consistency/completeness, listing required README updates, and noting claims to avoid.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Documents the single VITE_API_BASE_URL environment variable the frontend needs to reach the Hono backend at runtime.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry that mounts the React app into #root, declares favicons, SimTalk OpenGraph/Twitter metadata, and preloads Poppins/Luckiest Guy fonts.
nodes.languageNotes: Vite expects index.html at the project root; the module script tag points at src/main.tsx as the SPA entry.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend package manifest for @simtalk/frontend declaring React 19 + Vite 7 + LiveKit deps, the workspace dependency on @simtalk/shared-types, and dev/build/test scripts.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: Application TypeScript project extending the repo base config, enabling react-jsx and pulling in vite/client and vitest/globals ambient types over the src tree.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript solution file with no own files that references the app and node sub-projects for a single tsc -b composite build.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project for Node-side tooling — typechecks vite.config.ts under NodeNext module resolution with @types/node.

nodes.name: tsconfig.test.json
nodes.summary: Test TypeScript project extending the app config, mapping @simtalk/shared-types, React, LiveKit, and testing-library/vitest paths so unit/component tests in tests/frontend/ typecheck without bundling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TypeScript path aliases to resolve workspace and node_modules type declarations directly so vitest typechecks without a Vite resolver.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for @simtalk/shared-types declaring it a private ESM package that builds Zod schemas/types via tsc, exposes dist/index as the public entry, and depends on zod with vitest/typescript for dev.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript build config for the shared-types package extending the workspace base, emitting declarations and source maps from src/ to dist/ using NodeNext module resolution while excluding tests.

nodes.name: tsconfig.test.json
nodes.summary: Type-check-only TypeScript config for shared-types tests; extends the package tsconfig, includes the repo's tests/shared and vitest.config.ts, and pins vitest module paths for IDE/CI typechecking.

nodes.name: accessGate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the password-based access gate middleware, exercising 401/200 paths and ensuring protected routes require valid credentials.

nodes.name: liveKitRooms.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the LiveKit room/token service, verifying room name derivation, identity assignment, and JWT minting against shared config.

nodes.name: openAiRealtime.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the OpenAI realtime client-secret minting service, mocking fetch responses and asserting request shape, sanitized errors, and cache headers.

nodes.name: home.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end test for the home/lobby flow, stubbing the realtime token endpoint and browser WebRTC/audio APIs to validate translation session start/stop UX.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses page.addInitScript to inject browser-side mocks for MediaDevices, RTCPeerConnection, and AudioContext so the WebRTC pipeline is fully deterministic in CI.

nodes.name: setupTests.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest global setup for frontend tests, wiring jest-dom matchers and shared testing-library configuration.

nodes.name: createTestConfig
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds an AppConfig with sensible LiveKit/OpenAI defaults and shallow overrides for individual specs.

nodes.name: createTestConfig
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs an AppConfig fixture for OpenAI realtime service tests, with overridable fields for env-driven scenarios.

nodes.name: mockRealtimeTokenRoute
nodes.summary: Installs a Playwright route handler that intercepts POSTs to /realtime/token, records request payloads, and fulfills with a fake client secret response.

nodes.name: installBrowserRealtimeMocks
nodes.summary: Injects browser-side stubs (MediaDevices.getUserMedia, RTCPeerConnection, AudioContext, MediaRecorder) and OpenAI SDP route mocks so the e2e suite can drive the realtime translation flow without real WebRTC.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses page.addInitScript so the mocks run before any application JS, then patches navigator.mediaDevices and the WebRTC constructors via Object.defineProperty.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: React 19 + Vite browser UI: SimTalk brand primitives, mode/session screens, design tokens, the App entry state machine, the HTML host, and the frontend package build/runtime configuration.

layers.name: Realtime Session Client
layers.description: Browser-side orchestration that mints OpenAI Realtime and LiveKit credentials and runs translated WebRTC/LiveKit sessions directly against OpenAI and LiveKit, plus the access-gate flow that fronts every session.

layers.name: Backend Token Service
layers.description: Hono token-minting backend that issues short-lived OpenAI Realtime client secrets and LiveKit room JWTs without ever proxying audio, exposed through the local Node entry and the Vercel serverless adapter, with its CORS/access-gate/rate-limit/security-headers middleware and per-package build configuration.

layers.name: Shared Contracts
layers.description: @simtalk/shared-types package: Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types that are the source of truth for every cross-boundary realtime/token payload, plus its build and test configuration.

layers.name: Test Suites
layers.description: Repo-root Vitest unit/integration/component suites for backend, frontend, and shared types, the Playwright end-to-end spec and runner config, and the shared frontend test setup harness.

layers.name: Deployment & CI/CD
layers.description: Vercel deployment manifest and the two GitHub Actions workflows that run CI checks and ship the app to Vercel.

layers.name: Agentic OS & Tooling
layers.description: In-repo agent operating layer: scripts/agentic Python tools and their unit/smoke tests, the .agentic memory store and config, .cursor plans and skills, the Understand Anything ignore file and graph cache, and the agentic OS v2 plan.

layers.name: Project Documentation
layers.description: Human-facing project docs: top-level CLAUDE/PRD/README/System Architecture, the docs/superpowers plans and specs, the UX redesign plan, and the generated docs/_generated repo context snapshots.

layers.name: Workspace Configuration
layers.description: Repository-wide configuration that the per-package layers share: pnpm workspace manifest, root package.json, base TypeScript config, ESLint config, the stale shadcn components manifest, the Prettier ignore list, and the local Claude IDE settings.

tour.title: Project Overview & Brief
tour.description: Start with the project README and the agent-first PROJECT_BRIEF to absorb the SimTalk pitch in plain prose: a private realtime speech-to-speech translation app built on OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate, with three local modes (Listener, Turn-about, Practice) and Phase 1.5 two-person LiveKit remote rooms. These two documents establish the non-negotiable Phase 1 invariants — backend-only OPENAI_API_KEY, no audio/transcript persistence, strict CORS, mandatory security headers, and Zod validation at every boundary — that the rest of the tour will show enforced in code.

tour.title: Monorepo Workspace Layout
tour.description: SimTalk is a pnpm workspace with three packages — frontend, backend, and shared/types — wired together at the root. The workspace manifest declares the package set, the root package.json centralises lint/test/build scripts that always rebuild shared-types first, and tsconfig.base.json defines the strict TypeScript settings every package extends. Knowing this layout is essential before reading any individual package, because cross-package imports resolve through pnpm and shared-types is always a peer, never a dependency duplicate.

tour.title: Shared Zod Contracts
tour.description: Building on Step 1's invariant 'validate every cross-boundary payload with Zod from @simtalk/shared-types', this single file is the source of truth for every request/response shape exchanged between the browser, the Hono backend, and OpenAI/LiveKit. It declares Zod schemas for the realtime token, LiveKit room create/token, API errors, health, and translation modes, and exports inferred TypeScript types so backend and frontend stay structurally identical without duplicating definitions. Every later step that crosses a boundary will import from here.
tour.languageLesson: Zod schemas double as runtime validators and compile-time types: `z.infer<typeof Schema>` produces a TypeScript type guaranteed to match the validator. This is the idiomatic way to keep cross-boundary contracts in sync without manually maintaining parallel TS interfaces.

tour.title: Backend Composition & Entry Points
tour.description: The backend is a token-minting service only — it never sees audio. server.ts is the local dev entry that boots Hono on `@hono/node-server`, while api/index.ts wraps the same app for Vercel's Node serverless runtime, so production and local development share one app instance. app.ts is the composition root that wires middleware and mounts routes, and config.ts centralises the typed environment (OPENAI_API_KEY, ALLOWED_ORIGINS, LiveKit creds, gate passwords). Two entry points, one assembled app, one config: this is the spine the rest of the backend hangs off.

tour.title: Security Middleware & Access Gate
tour.description: These four middleware modules implement the Phase 1 security invariants in code. cors.ts strictly reflects ALLOWED_ORIGINS (never `*`); securityHeaders.ts sets the required CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/Referrer-Policy/X-Content-Type-Options on every response; rateLimit.ts applies an in-memory per-IP limiter to the token endpoint as a Phase 1 guardrail; and accessGate.ts enforces a shared X-Access-Password header using SHA-256 with timing-safe comparison so the private prototype is not openly accessible. Together they form the request-side defence-in-depth before any route handler runs.
tour.languageLesson: Timing-safe comparison (e.g. Node's `crypto.timingSafeEqual`) prevents timing side-channels in password checks: a naive `===` short-circuits on the first mismatched byte, leaking length and prefix information. Hashing both sides to fixed-length digests before comparing is the standard pattern.

tour.title: Token-Minting Routes & Services
tour.description: This is the heart of the backend. The realtime route validates a token request with the shared Zod schema and delegates to openAiRealtime.ts, which calls OpenAI's client-secret endpoint with an AbortController timeout and returns a short-lived secret — the server API key never leaves the backend. The rooms route does the equivalent for LiveKit, delegating to liveKitRooms.ts to ensure rooms exist and sign participant JWTs. health.ts gives a cheap liveness probe. Notice the consistent pattern: routes are thin (validate + call service), services own the external API contract, and both produce typed errors that get sanitised before reaching the client.

tour.title: Frontend Bootstrap & Design System
tour.description: Crossing into the browser, main.tsx mounts the React 19 app inside StrictMode and loads tokens.css, the single source of truth for SimTalk's design system: 8px spacing rhythm, semantic colour tokens, base typography, and `prefers-reduced-motion` handling. The brand primitives.tsx layer exposes accessible button/input/card components that every screen builds from, Icons.tsx provides the inline SVG icon set (lucide-style, never emojis in the UI), and languages.ts is the canonical list of supported BCP-47 language tags. Everything visual downstream extends these foundations.

tour.title: App State Machine & Local Mode Surfaces
tour.description: App.tsx is the top-level state machine that selects mode (Listener / Turn-about / Practice / Remote Room), language pair, and session lifecycle. Each local mode has its own surface component: ListenerSurface streams one-way translated audio (UN-style), TurnaboutSurface handles shared-device manual direction switching, and PracticeSurface supports deliberate-speech rehearsal. Summary.tsx renders the post-session transcript review across all modes. This is where Step 1's three Phase 1 workflows become concrete React components, each consuming the realtime session you'll see next.

tour.title: Realtime Translation Session (Browser ↔ OpenAI)
tour.description: This pair is the architectural payoff: realtimeTokenClient.ts POSTs to the backend /realtime/token endpoint (attaching the access-gate password header and validating the response with the shared Zod schema), then realtimeTranslationSession.ts opens an RTCPeerConnection directly to OpenAI using the minted client secret. The browser exchanges SDP with OpenAI, streams mic audio out, plays translated audio back, and exposes transcript deltas — all without the backend ever proxying audio. TranscriptSheet.tsx renders those deltas live. This is the WebRTC three-boundary flow (browser, token service, OpenAI) made real.
tour.languageLesson: WebRTC SDP exchange: the browser creates an `RTCPeerConnection`, calls `createOffer()` to produce an SDP string describing its media capabilities, POSTs it to the peer (here, OpenAI's realtime endpoint), and applies the returned answer via `setRemoteDescription()`. Once ICE candidates resolve, audio flows peer-to-peer — the backend stays out of the media path entirely.

tour.title: Remote Rooms via LiveKit & Access Gate UI
tour.description: Phase 1.5's two-person remote rooms reuse the token-minting pattern from Step 6 but against LiveKit Cloud. Lobby.tsx is the entry surface where one participant creates a room and shares the link; RemoteRoomSurface.tsx joins via liveKitRemoteRoomSession.ts, which manages local mic/camera tracks, subscribes to the remote participant's audio/video, and crucially hands the original audio stream to the Step 9 translation session so each side hears the other translated. AccessGateModal.tsx and accessGate.ts implement the matching browser side of Step 5's password gate, storing the credential in sessionStorage and raising AccessDeniedError on 401 to re-prompt.

tour.title: Test Suites Across the Stack
tour.description: Tests live at the repo root in tests/, mirroring the package boundaries: tests/shared/unit/index.test.ts pins the Zod schemas from Step 3, tests/backend/integration/routes/realtime.test.ts exercises the assembled Hono app and Step 6's token route with mocked OpenAI, tests/frontend/unit/realtimeTranslationSession.test.ts drives Step 9's session with fake MediaStream/RTCPeerConnection, tests/frontend/component/App.test.tsx renders the App state machine, and tests/e2e/home.spec.ts is a Playwright smoke for the lobby. This is the test pyramid in practice — broad shared/unit base, focused integration in the middle, narrow E2E at the top.

tour.title: Deployment & CI/CD
tour.description: The capstone: vercel.json declares the single Vercel project that serves the Vite-built frontend statically and routes /api/* to the api/index.ts adapter from Step 4, giving you frontend + serverless backend from one deploy. .github/workflows/ci.yml runs format, lint, typecheck, unit tests, build, and Playwright E2E on Node 22 + pnpm 10 for every push and PR. .github/workflows/vercel-deploy.yml is gated on CI green and pushes preview builds for branches and production builds for main via protected environments — closing the loop from local code in Step 4 to a live URL.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions composition: `on:` defines triggers (push/pull_request), `jobs:` run in parallel by default, and `needs:` enforces ordering between jobs — here the deploy workflow gates on the CI workflow's success via `workflow_run`. Protected environments add a second gate (required reviewers, branch restrictions, environment secrets) for production deploys.
